info.title: REST API
info.description:
# Welcome to Coinbase Exchange API
## Introduction
The Exchange Trading APIs allow institutions to place orders and access account information. The following API pages detail various REST API endpoints we offer for lower-frequency trading and general requests.
## Getting Started
To get started, please visit one of the following pages:
- [Authentication](/exchange/docs/rest-auth)
- [Rate Limits](/exchange/docs/rest-rate-limits)
- [Pagination](/exchange/docs/rest-pagination)
- [Status Codes](/exchange/docs/rest-requests)
- [Quickstart](/exchange/docs/getting-started)
## FIX API
- [FIX API reference](/exchange/docs/fix-connectivity)
## WebSocket API
- [WebSocket API reference](/exchange/docs/websocket-overview)

get.summary: Get all accounts for a profile
get.description: Get a list of trading accounts from the profile of the API key.

get.summary: Get a single account by id
get.description: Information for a single account. Use this endpoint when you know the account_id. API key must belong to the same profile as the account.

List the holds of an account that belong to the same profile as the API key. Holds are placed on an account for any active orders or pending withdraw requests. As an order is filled, the hold amount is updated. If an order is canceled, any remaining hold is removed. For withdrawals, the hold is removed after it is completed.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Used for pagination. Sets start cursor to `before` date.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Used for pagination. Sets end cursor to `after` date.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit on number of results to return.

get.description: Lists ledger activity for an account. This includes anything that would affect the accounts balance - transfers, trades, fees, etc.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: Returns list of ledger entries from this account id.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Search by minimum posted date time and is inclusive of time provided. Valid formats are either RFC3339, date or date time and must be after Unix Epoch time.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: Search by maximum posted date time and is inclusive of time provided. Valid formats are either RFC3339, date or date time and must be after Unix Epoch time.

parameters.name: before

parameters.name: after

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit on number of results to return.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: Returns list of transfers from this account id.

parameters.name: after

parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Address book identifier

get.description: Gets all the user's available Coinbase wallets (These are the wallets/accounts that are used for buying and selling on www.coinbase.com)

post.description: Generates a one-time crypto address for depositing crypto.

get.description: Gets all currency conversions (i.e. USD -> USDC) for a given profile

post.description: Converts funds from `from` currency to `to` currency. Funds are converted on the `from` account in the `profile_id` profile.

get.description: Gets a list of current conversion fee rates and trailing 30 day volume by currency pair
Gets a currency conversion by id (i.e. USD -> USDC).

Gets a list of all known currencies.
 Note: Not all currencies may be currently in use for trading.
Gets a single currency by id.

post.description: Deposits funds from a www.coinbase.com wallet to the specified `profile_id`.
Deposits funds from a linked external payment method to the specified `profile_id`.

get.description: Get fees rates and 30 days trailing volume.
Get a list of fills. A fill is a partial or complete match on a specific order.

parameters.name: order_id
parameters.description: limit to fills on a specific order. Either `order_id` or `product_id` is required.

parameters.name: product_id
parameters.description: limit to fills on a specific product. Either `order_id` or `product_id` is required.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit on number of results to return.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Used for pagination. Sets start cursor to `before` id.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Used for pagination. Sets end cursor to `after` id.

parameters.name: market_type
parameters.description: Market type which the order was filled in.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Search by minimum posted date time and is inclusive of time provided. Valid formats are either RFC3339, date or date time and must be after Unix Epoch time.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: Search by maximum posted date time and is inclusive of time provided. Valid formats are either RFC3339, date or date time and must be after Unix Epoch time.

get.description: Accepts zero or more loan IDs as input. If no loan IDs are specified, it returns all loans for the user. Otherwise it returns only the loan IDs specified.
Get a list of lendable assets, a map of assets we accept as collateral for loans, and the haircut weight.
List summaries of interest owed by asset
List interest rate history for a loan
List interest charges for a loan

This API summarizes lending for a given client. It calculates the overall loan balance, collateral level, and amounts available to borrow. It also returns any withdrawal restrictions in force on the client.

This API is similar to lending-overview but is used to preview the results of opening a new loan. The values returned in the preview response take the existing loans, collateral and the potential change being previewed into account. Note the preview request accepts native currency amounts as input.

post.description: This API triggers a loan open request. Funding is not necessarily instantaneous and there is no SLA. You are notified when funds have settled in your Exchange account. Loan open requests, once initiated, cannot be canceled.

get.description: Get available currencies and interest rates for new loans. All amounts returned by this API are notional values (i.e., USD).

post.description: Submit an interest repayment for a loan.
Submit a principal repayment for a loan.

get.description: Preview the results of a loan principal repayment.
Get the setting to automatically initiate loans to meet margin calls with Coinbase affiliates in accordance with the Lending Agreement and Side Letter

post.description: Automatically initiate loans to meet margin calls with Coinbase affiliates in accordance with the Lending Agreement and Side Letter

get.description: Get the USDC to USD auto conversion setting to meet margin calls with eligible Coinbase Affiliate

post.description: Set the USDC to USD auto conversion to meet margin calls with eligible Coinbase Affiliate

get.description: List your current open orders. Only open or un-settled orders are returned by default. As soon as an order is no longer open and settled, it will no longer appear in the default request. Open orders may change state between the request and the response depending on market conditions.

delete.description: With best effort, cancel all open orders. This may require you to make the request multiple times until all of the open orders are deleted.

200.description: A list of the `id`s of open orders that were successfully cancelled

schema.description: A list of the `id`s of open orders that were successfully cancelled

parameters.name: profile_id

parameters.name: product_id
parameters.description: Cancels orders on a specific product only

post.description: Create an order. You can place two types of orders: limit and market. Orders can only be placed if your account has sufficient funds. Once an order is placed, your account funds will be put on hold for the duration of the order. How much and which funds are put on hold depends on the order type and parameters specified.

200.description: The new order that was successfully created

schema.description: The new order that was successfully created

get.description: Get a single order by `id`.

200.description: the `id` of the order that was cancelled`

Get a list of open orders for a product. The amount of detail shown can be customized with the `level` parameter.
Historic rates for a product. Rates are returned in grouped buckets. Candle schema is of the form `[timestamp, price_low, price_high, price_open, price_close]`

get.description: Gets a list of all of the current user's profiles.

Transfer an amount of currency from one profile to another.

get.description: Information for a single profile. Use this endpoint when you know the profile_id.

put.description: Rename a profile. Names 'default' and 'margin' are reserved.
Deletes the profile specified by `profile_id` and transfers all funds to the profile specified by `to`. Fails if there are any open orders on the profile to be deleted.

Gets a list of in-progress and completed transfers of funds in/out of any of the user's accounts.

Get details for all redeems in the profile associated with the API key.

parameters.description: Used for pagination, returns stake-wraps with created_at timestamps newer than before date. Example date format 2023-03-15T23:40:04.505Z.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Used for pagination, returns stake-wraps with created_at timestamps older than after date. Example date format 2023-03-15T23:40:04.505Z.

parameters.description: Limit on number of results to return.

parameters.name: from_currency

Returns the circulating and total supply of a wrapped asset, and its conversion rate
Returns the conversion rate of a wrapped asset

is_verified_self_hosted_wallet.description: Flag to indicate if the crypto addresses has previously been digitally signed and verified when added in the Address Book UI tab

vasp_id.description: The VASP identifier if the address is owned by one of the supported Virtual Asset Service Providers

business_name.description: Business name of the originator's account - only populated for travel rules regions

business_country_code.description: The country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) of the originator's account location - only populated for travel rules regions

start_date.description: Start date for items to be included in report.

end_date.description: End date for items to be included in report

account_id.description: Account - Which account to generate the report for

is_verified_self_hosted_wallet.description: Flag to indicate if the crypto addresses has previously been digitally signed and verified when added in the Address Book UI tab. Required for crypto withdrawals in jurisdictions subjected to travel rules.

vasp_id.description: The VASP identifier if the address is owned by one of the supported Virtual Asset Service Providers. Required for crypto withdrawals in jurisdictions subjected to travel rules.

business_country_code.description: The country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) of the originator's account location.

is_certified_self_send.description: Flag to indicate if the user owns the crypto address

network.description: The blockchain name of the address. Required for crypto withdrawals in jurisdictions subject to travel rules.

is_verified_self_hosted_wallet.description: Flag to indicate if the crypto addresses has previously been digitally signed and verified when added in the Address Book UI tab

vasp_id.description: The VASP identifier if the address is owned by one of the supported Virtual Asset Service Providers

business_name.description: Business name of the originator's account

business_country_code.description: The country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) of the originator's account location.

destination_tag.description: Field for destination tag/memo for supported currencies. Ensure destination tag/memo is provided if sending to the address requires it

datetime.description: Designated date and time of the balance statement. Timezone is always UTC. If this field is empty, a report of the user’s current balance will be generated.

group_by_profile.description: Not applicable if generating report through an API key; only available through report generation via the Exchange user interface (UI).

warnings.title: Only send Orchid (OXT) to this address
warnings.details: Sending any other digital asset, including Ethereum (ETH), will result in permanent loss.

year.description: Year of the date. Must be from 1 to 9999.

month.description: Month of a year. Must be from 1 to 12.

day.description: Day of a month. Must be from 1 to 31 and valid for the year and month.

order_id.description: the `id` of the order that was cancelled`

body.description: A list of the `id`s of open orders that were successfully cancelled

usd_volume.description: The 30 days trailing volume in USD.

apiFees.description: Fees defines taker and maker fees for a given user including the volume in USD.

start_date.description: Start date for items to be included in report.

end_date.description: End date for items to be included in report

product_id.description: Product - Which product to generate the report for

apiGetFeesResponse.description: GetFeesResponse is used by the GET /fees.

current_owed.description: amount accrued in current month and not yet due

prior_period_overdue.description: interest payable that accrued in previous months and is now due

price.description: price per unit of base currency

size.description: amount of base currency to buy/sell

product_id.description: book the order was placed on

side.description: side of the book order is on - `buy` or `sell``

funds.description: amount of quote currency to spend (for market orders)

post_only.description: if true, forces order to be `maker` only

created_at.description: timestamp at which order was placed

done_at.description: timestamp at which order was done

done_reason.description: reason order was done (filled, rejected, or otherwise)

reject_reason.description: reason order was rejected by engine

fill_fees.description: fees paid on current filled amount

filled_size.description: amount (in base currency) of the order that has been filled

settled.description: true if funds have been exchanged and settled

stop_price.description: price (in quote currency) at which to execute the order

market_type.description: market type where order was traded

max_floor.description: maximum visible quantity for iceberg order

secondary_order_id.description: order id for the visible order for iceberg order

stop_limit_price.description: stop limit price for TPSL order

trade_id.description: id of trade that created the fill

product_id.description: book the order was placed on

liquidity.description: how did the order fill - M for maker or T for taker

price.description: price per unit of base currency

size.description: amount of base currency to buy/sell

fee.description: fees paid on current filled amount

side.description: side of the book order is on - `buy` or `sell``

settled.description: true if funds have been exchanged and settled

usd_volume.description: true if funds have been exchanged and settled

market_type.description: market type which the order was filled in

funding_currency.description: funding currency which the order was filled in

attest_verified_wallet_ownership.description: Required: user must attest that the wallet ownership was verified by the originating VASP

start_date.description: Start date for items to be included in report.

end_date.description: End date for items to be included in report

product_id.description: Product - Which product to generate the report for

profile_id.description: Create order on a specific `profile_id`. If none is passed, defaults to `default` profile.

type.description: Order type - `limit` orders can be `maker` or `taker`, `market` orders are always `taker`

side.description: Which side of the book to place order on

product_id.description: Book on which to place an order

stp.description: Choice of which self-trading prevention action engine will take. Options are `Decrease and Cancel`, `Cancel oldest`, `Cancel newest`, `Cancel both`

stop.description: Type of stop order to place. Requires `stop_price` to be present.

stop_price.description: Price threshold at which a `stop` order will be placed on the book

price.description: Price per unit of cryptocurrency - required for `limit`/`stop` orders

size.description: Amount of base currency to buy or sell - required for `limit`/`stop` orders and `market` `sell`s

funds.description: Amount of quote currency to buy - required for `market` `buy`s

time_in_force.description: How long the open order should be active for

cancel_after.description: When to automatically cancel the order (requires `time_in_force` to be passed in as `GTT`

post_only.description: If true, order will only execute as a `maker` order

client_oid.description: Optional Order ID selected by the user or the frontend client to identify their order

max_floor.description: Placing an iceberg order. Use this to specify how much to show

stop_limit_price.description: Required for take profit/stop loss orders. Denotes the updated limit price upon the activation of the stop loss trigger

body.description: The new order that was successfully created

type.description: Type of report to generate. Possible values: [`account`, `balance`, `fills`, `otc-fills`, `rfq-fills`, `tax-invoice`, `1099k-transaction-history`]

email.description: Email to send generated report notification to

profile_id.description: If this field is specified, it must be the `profile_id` that is linked to the API key.

address.description: Crypto address where funds will be deposited from

originator_name.description: Name of the originator of the funds

originator_country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 formatted country code of the originator of the funds

vasp_country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 formatted country code of the VASP

lei_number.description: Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) of the VASP

wallet_type.description: Type of wallet - Exchange or Self-hosted

is_self_certified.description: True if the user owns the wallet

user_id.description: The ID of the user who owns the account

high_bid_limit_percentage.description: Percentage to calculate highest price for limit buy order (Stable coin trading pair only)

addresses.description: List of addresses to add to the address book

add_network_fee_to_total.description: A boolean flag to add the network fee on top of the amount. If this is blank, it will default to deducting the network fee from the amount.

is_intermediary.description: A boolean flag to create a transfer using Coinbase as an intermediary VASP. If `true`, `intermediary_jurisdiction` must be provided. `travel_rule_data` may be necessary if the jurisdiction requires data.

travel_rule_data.description: The necessary data to satisfy the Travel Rule data requirements.

apiPublicPostWrappedAssetRedeemRequest.title:
Override PostWrappedAssetRedeemRequest with PublicPostWrappedAssetRedeemRequest in cloud.proto so we
don't expose non-public to public facing documentation

apiPublicPostWrappedAssetStakeWrapReques.title:
Override PostWrappedAssetStakeWrapRequest with PublicPostWrappedAssetStakeWrapRequest in cloud.proto so we
don't expose non-public to public facing documentation

start_date.description: Start date for items to be included in report.

end_date.description: End date for items to be included in report

product_id.description: Product - Which product to generate the report for

start_date.description: Start date for items to be included in report.

end_date.description: End date for items to be included in report

product_id.description: Product - Which product to generate the report for

created_at.description: Timestamp of when entry was added

address.description: Crypto address where funds will be deposited from

originator_name.description: Name of the originator of the funds

originator_country.description: country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) of the originator of the funds

vasp_country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 formatted country code of the VASP

lei_number.description: Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) of the VASP

originator_name.description: Required: name of the originator sending the funds

originator_natural_name.description: The given name and surname of the originator as defined in a government issued ID.

originator_address.description: Required: address of the originator sending the funds, address_1, city, country are required fields

originator_account.description: An account identifier for the originator.

originator_account_location_country_code.description: The country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) of the originator's account location.

originator_account_number.description: Required: account number of the originator sending the funds.

beneficiary_name.description: Required: name of the beneficiary receiving the funds

beneficiary_address.description: Required: address of the beneficiary receiving the funds, address_1, city, country are required fields

beneficiary_telephone_number.description: The telephone number of the beneficiary.

beneficiary_account_location.description: The country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) of the beneficiary's account location.

beneficiary_financial_institution.description: The beneficiary financial institution, e.g. Coinbase.

is_self.description: Required: whether the user claims the receiving wallet belongs to the originator

originator_wallet_address.description: The crypto address of the originator.

originating_vasp_for_intermediary.description: Optional: fields for supporting intermediary flow

apiUnauthorizedResponse.description:
UnauthorizedResponse is the response message for endpoints in rest-gateway that requires authentication.
This message is used to generate the Exchange REST API documentation using OpenAPI format.

id.description: The symbol of the wrapped asset

circulating_supply.description: The assets wrapped by customers less the assets unwrapped by customers existing outside of Coinbase's premint account

total_supply.description: The total token supply of the asset matching that on Etherscan

conversion_rate.description: The conversion rate between the wrapped asset and the underlying asset

apy.description: The APY earned by the supply of the underlying asset

redeem_time_estimate_days.description: The estimated time to redeem the wrapped asset in days

protobufNullValue.description:
`NullValue` is a singleton enumeration to represent the null value for the
`Value` type union.

The JSON representation for `NullValue` is JSON `null`.

 - NULL_VALUE: Null value.
